title: Weather Normalization Negatively Affecting Rates
summary:
Main
Freight futures across the board took a dive today, as the tightness and caution of last week due to a couple of weather systems in North China that were causing delays, faded. With the market now normalizing, a number of market participants felt more comfortable to hedge forward positions with futures that have been relatively stable for the past few weeks and, as a result, selling pressure across all asset classes pushed futures at the low end of their recent trading range. Yet, we don't foresee further aggressive moves on freight futures for now, despite the fact that the physical market remains relatively inactive with a downwards bias which is limiting the optimism in the futures market.
